qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in file pull endpoint permits arbitrary URL access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The SSRF finding targets a different vulnerable component (Panel PHP FileController.php file pull endpoint at /api/client/servers/{server}/files/pull) with a different root cause (missing URL validation and SSRF protection) compared to the advisory which describes a resource exhaustion DoS vulnerability in Wings Go code (internal/cron/activity_cron.go:81 and internal/cron/sftp_cron.go:86) caused by not considering SQLite max parameter limits when deleting processed activity log entries.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-2497-gp99-2m74, CVE-2026-21696) describes a resource exhaustion DoS vulnerability in Pterodactyl Wings Go code where the activity cron job does not consider SQLite max parameter limits (32766) when deleting processed activity log entries, causing entries to be endlessly reprocessed and re-sent to the panel until disk space is exhausted (CWE-400/CWE-770). The only qualifying finding (ssrf-file-pull) identifies an SSRF vulnerability in the Panel PHP file pull endpoint with a completely different root cause (missing URL validation), vulnerable component (Panel vs Wings), programming language (PHP vs Go), and vulnerability class (SSRF vs resource exhaustion).
evidence: Advisory GHSA-2497-gp99-2m74 describes a DoS vulnerability in Wings Go code: internal/cron/activity_cron.go:81 and internal/cron/sftp_cron.go:86, where SQLite max parameter limits are not considered during activity log entry deletion, causing endless reprocessing and disk space exhaustion.

The only qualifying finding (ssrf-file-pull) targets the Panel PHP file pull endpoint (FileController.php:250, DaemonFileRepository.php:278) with missing URL validation/SSRF protection, which is a different component (Panel vs Wings), different language (PHP vs Go), different root cause (missing URL validation vs missing SQLite parameter limit check), and different vulnerability class (SSRF/CWE-918 vs resource exhaustion/CWE-400,770).

The other two findings are non_qualifying_vuln: code-injection-egg-import (PR:H - requires admin) and command-injection-console (AT:P - depends on game server software). Neither matches the advisory root cause.
